seoTitle: Injoinic Solutions - Fast Charging & Power Management Applications
seoDescription: Explore Injoinic application solutions for power banks, fast chargers, wireless charging, and battery management systems. LiTong provides complete design support.

faqs.question: What application solutions does Injoinic provide?
faqs.answer: Injoinic provides comprehensive solutions for power management and fast charging applications. Their solutions cover power banks with multi-protocol fast charging, wall adapters supporting various fast charging standards, wireless charging pads and receivers, and battery management systems for lithium battery packs. Each solution includes reference designs, BOM recommendations, and application guidance to accelerate product development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solution categories to find the right application for your project. Contact us for detailed design support.

faqs.question: How can LiTong support my Injoinic-based design?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Injoinic-based designs including reference designs and application notes, schematic and PCB layout review, component selection guidance, sample provision for evaluation, technical troubleshooting, and production support. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Injoinic products and can help optimize your design for performance, cost, and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design phase for optimal support. We can provide reference designs and review your implementation.

faqs.question: What is included in Injoinic reference designs?
faqs.answer: Injoinic reference designs include complete schematics with component values, PCB layout files and guidelines, BOM with recommended suppliers, application notes explaining design choices, test reports with performance data, and firmware examples where applicable. These reference designs are tested and validated, providing a solid foundation for your product development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request reference designs from our team. They provide an excellent starting point for your development.

faqs.question: Can Injoinic solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Injoinic solutions can be customized for specific application requirements. Modifications may include adjusting output voltage and current levels, adding or removing supported protocols, optimizing for specific form factors, integrating additional features, and adapting for different battery configurations. Our FAE team can guide you through customization options while maintaining reliability and compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Discuss your specific requirements with our FAE team. We can recommend appropriate customizations.

faqs.question: What certifications do Injoinic reference designs meet?
faqs.answer: Injoinic reference designs are developed with certification requirements in mind. They typically comply with USB-IF specifications for PD implementations, Qi standards for wireless charging, and safety standards for the target markets. However, final product certification is the responsibility of the product manufacturer. We provide guidance on meeting certification requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for certification early in design. We can provide guidance on meeting relevant standards.

solutions.name: Multi-Protocol Fast Charging Power Bank
solutions.title: Multi-Protocol Fast Charging Power Bank Solution
solutions.description: Complete power bank solution supporting PD, QC, SCP, FCP and other fast charging protocols with high-efficiency power conversion.
solutions.longDescription:
The Multi-Protocol Fast Charging Power Bank from injoinic represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of injoinic components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: Supports all major fast charging protocols for universal compatibility
High-efficiency power conversion maximizes battery runtime
Compact design with minimal external components
Comprehensive protection ensures safety and reliability
Proven reference design accelerates time to market

coreAdvantages.title: Universal Protocol Support
coreAdvantages.description: Automatic detection and support for PD, QC, SCP, FCP, AFC and more protocols, ensuring compatibility with smartphones, tablets, and laptops from all major manufacturers.

coreAdvantages.title: High Efficiency Conversion
coreAdvantages.description: Synchronous buck-boost converter achieves up to 96% efficiency, maximizing battery capacity utilization and extending device runtime.

coreAdvantages.title: Intelligent Power Management
coreAdvantages.description: Integrated battery management with automatic charging, discharging control, and protection functions simplifies design and improves reliability.

coreAdvantages.title: Compact Integration
coreAdvantages.description: Highly integrated ICs reduce BOM component count and PCB size, enabling slim and portable power bank designs.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Protection
coreAdvantages.description: Built-in over-voltage, over-current, short-circuit, and temperature protection ensures safe operation under all conditions.

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a cost-effective power bank solution supporting multiple fast charging protocols for the mid-range market. Their existing solution used discrete components from multiple vendors, resulting in high BOM cost and complex manufacturing. They required a solution that could support PD, QC, and SCP protocols while maintaining competitive pricing.
customerCases.solution: We recommended the Injoinic IP2723T + IP6538 solution, providing a highly integrated design with minimal external components. The reference design was customized to support 18W output with dual USB ports (1x USB-C + 1x USB-A). Our FAE team provided schematic review and PCB layout guidance to optimize thermal performance.
result: Reduced BOM cost by 35% compared to discrete solution
Achieved 94% average conversion efficiency
Passed all compatibility tests with major smartphone brands
Successfully launched product with 500K units shipped in first year
Customer satisfaction rate exceeded 98%
customerCases.feedback: The Injoinic solution exceeded our expectations in both performance and cost. LiTong's technical support was instrumental in optimizing our design for manufacturing. The protocol compatibility is excellent - our power bank works with virtually every device our customers have tested.

customerCases.challenge: The customer required an ultra-slim power bank design (8mm thickness) for a premium smartphone accessory line. The design constraints included minimal PCB space, strict thermal requirements, and support for 15W PD fast charging. Traditional solutions were too bulky or inefficient for this form factor.
customerCases.solution: We proposed a compact design using IP2723T in QFN-24 package paired with a high-efficiency IP6538 converter. The design utilized a 4-layer PCB with optimized copper pours for heat dissipation. Our team provided detailed layout guidelines to minimize EMI and maximize thermal performance in the constrained space.
result: Achieved 7.8mm thickness meeting design target
Delivered 15W PD output with 92% efficiency
Surface temperature remained below 45°C during operation
Passed CE, FCC, and UL certifications on first attempt
Design won Red Dot Design Award 2023
customerCases.feedback: LiTong's expertise in compact power design was crucial to our success. The Injoinic ICs provided the perfect balance of integration and performance for our ultra-slim design. The thermal management guidance helped us achieve excellent performance in a challenging form factor.

faeInsights.content: The IP2723T + IP6538 combination represents an optimal solution for modern power bank designs requiring multi-protocol support. The key insight is that integration level directly impacts both cost and reliability - fewer components mean fewer failure points and lower manufacturing costs. When implementing this solution, focus on three critical areas: thermal management of the buck-boost converter, proper layout of the Type-C CC lines for reliable protocol detection, and careful selection of the power path MOSFETs for minimal voltage drop. The protocol IC's automatic detection capability eliminates complex firmware development, but proper PCB layout of the CC pins is essential for reliable operation. For battery protection, always include a dedicated protection IC rather than relying solely on the converter's protection features.
faeInsights.logic: The design decision framework for power bank development follows this sequence: First, determine the target output power and supported protocols based on market requirements. Second, calculate the battery capacity needed considering conversion efficiency and target device charge cycles. Third, select the converter topology - buck-boost is preferred for single-cell designs to maintain consistent output as battery voltage drops. Fourth, choose the protocol IC based on required protocol support and integration level. Fifth, design the thermal management system based on maximum power dissipation calculations. Finally, implement comprehensive protection including battery, thermal, and fault protection.
keyTakeaways: Integration reduces BOM cost by 30-40% compared to discrete solutions
Thermal design is critical - plan for worst-case continuous operation
Protocol compatibility testing with real devices is essential before production
Battery protection IC is mandatory for safety certification
Layout of CC lines affects protocol detection reliability
commonPitfalls: Insufficient copper area for heat dissipation leading to thermal shutdown
Improper CC line routing causing intermittent protocol detection
Inadequate input/output capacitance resulting in voltage ripple
Undersized inductor causing efficiency loss and heating
bestPractices: Use 4-layer PCB with dedicated ground plane for optimal thermal and EMI performance
Place input capacitors as close as possible to converter IC
Implement proper CC line impedance matching for reliable PD communication
Include test points for debugging protocol and power issues
Design for manufacturability with adequate component spacing

faqs.question: What is the maximum output power of this power bank solution?
faqs.answer: The standard solution supports up to 18W output power (9V/2A or 12V/1.5A). This is limited by the IP6538 converter capability and thermal considerations in typical power bank enclosures. For higher power requirements (30W+), consider using external MOSFETs with the IP6538 controller or alternative converter solutions. The actual sustained power may be lower depending on thermal design and ambient temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For standard applications, 18W is sufficient for most smartphones and tablets. Contact us for higher power solution recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I optimize thermal performance in compact designs?
faqs.answer: Thermal optimization requires attention to several factors: Use multilayer PCBs with thermal vias connecting to inner ground planes. Place thermal vias under the converter IC's exposed pad. Maximize copper area on all layers for heat spreading. Use shielded inductors with low DCR to minimize conduction losses. Position heat-generating components away from temperature-sensitive parts. Consider thermal interface materials if the enclosure allows. Test under worst-case conditions (maximum load, highest ambient temperature) to verify thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Thermal simulation is recommended for compact designs. We can provide thermal design guidelines and review your layout.

faqs.question: Can this solution support pass-through charging?
faqs.answer: Yes, the solution supports pass-through charging where the output remains active while charging the internal battery. The IP6538 handles power path management, prioritizing output power while using available current to charge the battery. This requires proper configuration of the power path MOSFETs and control logic. The reference design includes pass-through functionality with automatic power flow management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Pass-through is supported in the reference design. Enable this feature in your firmware if required.

faqs.question: What battery cells are compatible with this solution?
faqs.answer: The solution is designed for single-cell lithium-ion or lithium-polymer batteries with nominal voltage of 3.7V and full charge voltage of 4.2V. Compatible cell formats include 18650 cylindrical cells, 21700 cells, and various polymer pouch cells. Battery capacity can range from 1000mAh to 20000mAh or more. The IP3005 protection IC provides appropriate protection for these cell types. For other battery chemistries or configurations, contact us for alternative solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard 3.7V lithium cells. Select capacity based on your product size and runtime requirements.

faqs.question: How do I implement LED battery level indication?
faqs.answer: The reference design includes a 4-LED battery level indicator driven by the IP2723T or an external MCU. Each LED represents approximately 25% of battery capacity. The IC monitors battery voltage and drives the LEDs accordingly. During charging, LEDs sequentially light up to show progress. The LED control can be customized for different indication patterns or brightness levels. The reference schematic shows the recommended LED circuit with current-limiting resistors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow the reference design for LED indication. Customize LED patterns in firmware if needed.

faqs.question: What safety certifications are required for power banks?
faqs.answer: Power banks typically require multiple safety certifications depending on target markets: CE marking for European markets, FCC for USA, UL or cUL for safety compliance, PSE for Japan, and CCC for China. The battery cells must have UN38.3 certification for transportation. Injoinic ICs are designed to meet safety requirements, but final product certification is the manufacturer's responsibility. Our reference designs include safety considerations, but you must test and certify your specific product implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for required certifications early in design. We can provide guidance on meeting safety standards.

solutions.name: Qi Wireless Charging Pad
solutions.title: Qi Wireless Charging Pad Solution
solutions.description: Complete Qi-compliant wireless charging solution supporting up to 15W fast wireless charging with advanced FOD and safety features.
solutions.longDescription:
The Qi Wireless Charging Pad from injoinic represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of injoinic components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: Qi-compliant design ensures universal device compatibility
Up to 15W fast wireless charging support
Advanced FOD prevents heating from foreign objects
Automatic power optimization based on device requirements
Compact design suitable for various form factors

coreAdvantages.title: Universal Qi Compatibility
coreAdvantages.description: Compliant with WPC Qi standard supporting BPP and EPP profiles, ensuring compatibility with iPhones, Samsung Galaxy, Google Pixel, and all other Qi-enabled devices.

coreAdvantages.title: Fast Wireless Charging
coreAdvantages.description: Supports up to 15W EPP fast wireless charging for compatible devices, delivering charging speeds comparable to wired fast charging.

coreAdvantages.title: Advanced Safety Features
coreAdvantages.description: Integrated foreign object detection (FOD) and temperature monitoring prevent unsafe operation, protecting both the charger and devices.

coreAdvantages.title: Intelligent Power Control
coreAdvantages.description: Automatic power adjustment based on receiver communication ensures optimal charging efficiency and device safety.

coreAdvantages.title: Simplified Design
coreAdvantages.description: High integration reduces external component count, enabling compact and cost-effective wireless charging pad designs.

customerCases.challenge: The customer wanted to enter the wireless charging market with a premium 15W fast charging pad. They required a solution that could compete with established brands in terms of charging speed, safety features, and design aesthetics. The challenge included achieving Qi certification, implementing reliable FOD, and maintaining competitive BOM cost.
customerCases.solution: We recommended the Injoinic IP6808-based solution with comprehensive reference design support. Our team provided coil design guidance, PCB layout review, and FOD tuning assistance. The design incorporated premium materials and a sleek form factor while maintaining cost competitiveness through Injoinic's high integration.
result: Achieved 15W fast charging with 78% end-to-end efficiency
Passed Qi certification on first submission
FOD successfully detected all tested foreign objects without false triggers
Product launched at competitive price point with 40% margin
4.5-star average customer rating on major retail platforms
customerCases.feedback: The Injoinic IP6808 provided the perfect foundation for our wireless charging product. LiTong's support during Qi certification was invaluable - their expertise helped us pass on the first attempt. The FOD performance is excellent, giving our customers confidence in product safety.

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a wireless charging solution for automotive applications with strict requirements for temperature range, vibration resistance, and EMI compliance. The solution needed to support 10W charging in varying vehicle electrical environments and operate reliably in temperatures from -20°C to +70°C.
customerCases.solution: We customized the IP6808 solution for automotive requirements, adding robust input protection for vehicle electrical transients, enhanced thermal management for high-temperature operation, and EMI filtering for automotive EMC compliance. Our FAE team worked closely with the customer's engineering team through design verification and testing.
result: Successfully operated across full automotive temperature range
Passed automotive EMC testing (CISPR 25 Class 5)
Survived rigorous vibration and shock testing
Achieved 10W stable charging in all vehicle conditions
Adopted by major automotive OEM as approved accessory
customerCases.feedback: LiTong's automotive expertise and Injoinic's robust IC platform enabled us to meet demanding automotive requirements. The solution has proven extremely reliable in field deployment. We appreciate the dedicated support throughout the development and qualification process.

faeInsights.content: Wireless charging design requires attention to three critical areas: coil design, power stage efficiency, and FOD reliability. The coil is the heart of the system - its inductance, Q factor, and construction directly impact efficiency and compatibility. For the IP6808 solution, use a properly designed A11 coil with 6.3μH inductance and high-quality Litz wire. The power stage efficiency depends on MOSFET selection and resonant capacitor quality - use low-RDS(on) MOSFETs and high-Q film capacitors. FOD tuning is crucial for safety and user experience. The IP6808's advanced FOD algorithm requires proper calibration for your specific coil and mechanical design. Test with various foreign objects (coins, keys, credit cards) to verify reliable detection without false triggers during normal charging.
faeInsights.logic: The wireless charging design process follows this framework: First, define the target power level and Qi profile based on application requirements. Second, design or select the transmitter coil following WPC specifications for inductance and dimensions. Third, design the resonant tank with appropriate capacitance for the operating frequency. Fourth, select power stage components (MOSFETs, drivers) based on current and efficiency requirements. Fifth, implement FOD calibration for your specific mechanical design. Sixth, design the control and communication circuits. Finally, conduct thorough testing including efficiency measurement, FOD verification, and interoperability testing with various receiver devices.
keyTakeaways: Coil quality is critical - invest in proper Litz wire and construction
FOD calibration is essential for each unique mechanical design
Qi certification requires testing with certified test equipment
Thermal management affects both safety and efficiency
EMI filtering is necessary for regulatory compliance
commonPitfalls: Using copper wire instead of Litz wire, causing excessive AC losses
Insufficient FOD calibration leading to false triggers or missed objects
Inadequate coil shielding causing interference with device electronics
Poor thermal design resulting in temperature derating
Missing EMI filtering causing regulatory compliance failures
bestPractices: Use certified Qi coils or follow WPC design guidelines exactly
Implement comprehensive FOD testing with various object types
Include adequate ferrite shielding between coil and electronics
Design for thermal management with temperature monitoring
Plan for Qi certification early with pre-testing using certified equipment

faqs.question: What is the difference between BPP and EPP in wireless charging?
faqs.answer: BPP (Baseline Power Profile) provides up to 5W wireless charging and is the basic Qi standard. EPP (Extended Power Profile) enables faster charging up to 15W by allowing higher power transfer and more efficient communication between transmitter and receiver. EPP requires more sophisticated control and better coil coupling. The IP6808 supports both profiles, automatically negotiating the appropriate power level with the connected device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Support both BPP and EPP for maximum compatibility. EPP devices will charge faster when using compatible transmitters.

faqs.question: How do I select the right transmitter coil?
faqs.answer: Transmitter coil selection depends on your application requirements. For standard smartphone charging, use a 50mm diameter A11 coil with 6.3μH inductance. For smaller devices like wearables, consider smaller coils (30-40mm). For multi-device or free-position charging, use multi-coil arrays. Key parameters are inductance (must be 6.3μH ±10% for Qi compliance), Q factor (higher is better for efficiency), and construction (use Litz wire for high-frequency performance). Source coils from reputable suppliers who understand Qi requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard A11 coils for most applications. Contact us for coil supplier recommendations and custom coil design.

faqs.question: What causes wireless charging to be slower than expected?
faqs.answer: Slow wireless charging can result from several factors: Poor coil alignment reduces power transfer efficiency - ensure proper positioning. Foreign objects between coils trigger power reduction for safety. High ambient temperature causes thermal derating. Incompatible or non-Qi-certified devices may not support fast charging. Low-quality power adapters cannot supply sufficient input power. Phone cases thicker than 3-4mm reduce coupling efficiency. The IP6808 automatically optimizes power, but these factors can limit maximum charging speed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure proper alignment and remove thick cases. Use a quality power adapter rated for required input power.

faqs.question: How do I test FOD functionality?
faqs.answer: Test FOD by placing various metallic objects on the charging pad and verifying that charging does not start or stops immediately. Test objects should include coins, keys, metal pens, and credit cards. Also verify that legitimate devices are not falsely detected as foreign objects. Test across the entire charging surface as FOD sensitivity may vary with position. Document all test results for Qi certification. The IP6808 allows FOD sensitivity adjustment to optimize for your mechanical design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Conduct comprehensive FOD testing with various objects. Adjust sensitivity if needed for your design.

faqs.question: What input power adapter should I use?
faqs.answer: The input power adapter must provide sufficient power for your maximum output plus conversion losses. For 15W wireless output, use a QC 3.0 or PD adapter capable of 9V/2A or 12V/1.5A (18W+). For 10W output, a 9V/1.5A (15W) adapter is sufficient. For 5W basic charging, a standard 5V/2A USB adapter works. The IP6808 works with both QC and PD adapters - use IP2723T for automatic protocol detection and negotiation. Include input protection for over-voltage and transient suppression.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match adapter power to your wireless output requirements. Use QC or PD adapters for fast wireless charging.

faqs.question: How do I achieve Qi certification for my wireless charger?
faqs.answer: Qi certification requires testing by a WPC-authorized test lab. The process includes: Registering your product with WPC, submitting samples for compliance testing against Qi specifications, passing interoperability testing with reference receivers, and paying certification fees. Prepare by using Qi-compliant transmitter ICs (like IP6808), following WPC coil design guidelines, and conducting pre-testing with Qi test equipment. The certification process typically takes 4-8 weeks. LiTong can provide guidance on preparing for certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start certification process early. Use Qi-compliant components and follow WPC guidelines. We can provide certification support.
